title: Coq au vin – chicken with a kick

seo.title: Coq au vin - chicken with a kick - Marinda Kook

html:
Coq Au Vin remains one of the tastiest chicken dishes there is. It's not much trouble, but it always gets lots of compliments. Lovely with bread or just plain white rice.

 

YOU'LL NEED;

  • 8 chicken thighs or 1 whole chicken
  • 125 ml cake flour
  • 60 ml butter
  • 30 ml oil
  • 15 small whole baby onions
  • 1 packet bacon ( I use the ready-diced bacon)
  • 125 ml brandy
  • 1 bottle red wine
  • 5 garlic cloves
  • 3 sprigs thyme
  • 2 bay leaves
  • 1 packet mushrooms
  • salt and pepper
  • parsley for garnishing

NOW:

  1. fry your bacon until brown
  2. fry the onions along with the bacon – once browned, scoop them out and set aside
  3. pour the flour into a plastic bag and shake the chicken in it until it's fully coated with the flour
  4. brown the chicken in the same pot
  5. pour the brandy over the chicken and flambé it – just watch your eyebrows
  6. add the bacon, onions and the rest of the ingredients except the mushrooms
  7. put the lid on and let it simmer for 60 minutes
  8. add the mushrooms and let it simmer for a further 30 minutes
  9. taste and add salt and pepper to taste

Serve it over rice and with vegetables of your choice. To peel the onions easily – put the onions in a bowl and pour hot water over them. Let it stand for about 10 minutes and the onions peel off very easily. A little bit of French cooking on your table.
